theme: Ambient Intelligence — The Office That Notices You

ideas.title: Idle Workers Wander the Office
ideas.description: Right now idle workers sit at their desks doing nothing — they're assigned a desk slot just like working workers. When a worker is idle, instead of parking them at a desk, give them a slow CSS wander animation. Add a new div 'wanderer' that floats around the office floor with a gentle random drift using CSS keyframes (translateX/Y with 20-30s duration, different delays per worker). Show their avatar at 70% opacity with a thought bubble ('💭 thinking...', '☕ brb', '📱 scrolling'). This makes the office feel alive even when nobody's shipping — you can SEE who's between tasks vs who's grinding. WHY: The biggest visual gap right now is that idle workers are invisible (they're just ghost desks). Making them wander creates ambient life and a clear visual distinction between 'working at desk' and 'taking a break'.

ideas.title: Confetti Particles Match Worker's Role Color
ideas.description: The completion confetti (App.jsx) currently uses a fixed color palette. When a worker completes a task, the confetti should use that worker's WORKER_ROLE_COLORS value as the dominant color (60% of particles), with white and gold as accent (20% each). Pass the completing worker's name to the confetti generator and look up their color. This is a 5-line change in the confetti spawn logic. WHY: Right now all completions feel the same. When Maya ships and purple confetti flies, vs Dave shipping with orange confetti, each completion has identity. You can tell WHO just shipped without reading anything.

ideas.title: Office Ambient Sound Layer (Optional Toggle)
ideas.description: Add a tiny speaker icon in the corner (🔇/🔊) that toggles ambient office sounds. Use the Web Audio API to generate simple procedural audio: soft white noise filtered to sound like air conditioning, occasional soft 'click' sounds (keyboard), and a gentle chime on task completion. No audio files needed — all procedural via OscillatorNode and filtered noise. Default OFF (muted). Store preference in localStorage. WHY: Sound transforms passive watching into immersive presence. Even at very low volume, ambient office noise makes the dashboard feel like a real place you're visiting. The procedural approach means zero payload increase.

ideas.title: Mini Timeline Bar Below Office Floor
ideas.description: Add a thin horizontal bar (height: 6px) below the office floor that shows the last 24 hours of activity as colored segments. Each completed task is a small colored block (worker's role color) plotted by timestamp on a 24h timeline. Errors are red dots. Gaps are transparent. Build from activityEntries already available as a prop. Render as a single div with positioned child spans. On hover, show a tooltip with the task details. WHY: The activity log is text and requires scrolling. A timeline gives an instant visual answer to 'how busy was the office today?' — a single glance shows if the team shipped 2 things or 20. It's the kind of data visualization that makes people keep the tab open.

ideas.title: Pulsing Glow on Active Desks
ideas.description: Working desks have class 'desk--active' but the only visual signal is the lamp and the character. Add a subtle pulsing glow ring around active desks using box-shadow with the worker's --role-color CSS variable (already set on the desk element). Use @keyframes desk-pulse { 0%,100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(var(--role-color-rgb), 0.3) } 50% { box-shadow: 0 0 8px 3px rgba(var(--role-color-rgb), 0.15) } } with a slow 3s infinite animation. This creates a breathing effect that makes active desks stand out without being distracting. WHY: The office floor is hard to scan quickly. A gentle pulse on active desks creates an immediate visual hierarchy — your eye goes to where work is happening.

ideas.title: Keyboard Shortcut: Press 1-6 to Focus Worker Cards
ideas.description: Add a keydown listener on the document: pressing 1-6 focuses the corresponding worker card (scrolls to it, applies the existing worker-card--focused class). Press Escape to clear focus. Press 'o' to toggle between Office and Dashboard tabs. This costs almost nothing to implement and gives power users a way to navigate without clicking. Show a tiny '?' icon in the header that reveals the shortcuts on hover. WHY: Anyone who leaves this dashboard open all day is a power user. Keyboard shortcuts are the love language of power users.

ideas.title: Office Pet: A Cat That Walks Across Desks
ideas.description: Add a tiny pixel-art cat (pure CSS, 12x8px) that slowly walks across the office floor on a randomized path. It uses CSS animations with a 60-second loop, moving between desk positions. When it reaches a working character, it 'sits' for 10 seconds (swap to sitting sprite). The cat appears only when the team vibe is 'in-flow' or 'crushing' — when the vibe is 'on-fire', the cat hides under a desk. Give it a name (Pixel) and a hover tooltip: 'Pixel the office cat. Morale officer.' WHY: Every great office has an unofficial mascot. A cat that reacts to team vibe is pure delight — it gives visitors something to watch during quiet moments and creates emergent narratives ('oh the cat is hiding, things must be intense'). It's the feature people would screenshot and share.
